text: Many workplaces now use artificial intelligence to handle repetitive tasks. Automation takes over data entry, scheduling, and basic customer queries, freeing employees for more creative work. Productivity tools like smart email assistants and document summarizers help workers finish tasks faster. However, human-AI collaboration is key: people still make final decisions, solve complex problems, and provide emotional support. Some jobs are changing rather than disappearing. For example, factory workers may now monitor robotic systems instead of manual assembly. Preparing for these shifts means learning to work alongside AI, focusing on skills that machines cannot easily replace.
